channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Ilia Malinin's Olympic backflip made history. But he's not the first to do it
item.description: U.S. figure skating phenom Ilia Malinin did a backflip in his Olympic debut, and another the next day. The controversial move was banned from competit...

item.title: Immigration courts fast-track hearings for Somali asylum claims
item.description: Their lawyers fear the notices are merely the first step toward the removal without due process of Somali asylum applicants in the country.

item.title: 'End of Days' recalls the violent 1992 Ruby Ridge confrontation in Idaho
item.description: Author Chris Jennings talks the apocalyptic religious views that fueled the standoff between federal agents and the family of Randy Weaver — and the u...

item.title: Japan's Takaichi to pursue conservative agenda after election landslide
item.description: Japan's first female Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, brought the ruling Liberal Democratic Party its biggest-ever electoral victory, fueling her ambit...

item.title: Nancy Guthrie search enters its second week as a purported deadline looms
item.description: "This is very valuable to us, and we will pay," Savannah Guthrie said in a new video message, seeking to communicate with people who say they're holdi...

item.title: Ghislaine Maxwell appeals for clemency from Trump as she declines to answer questions from lawmakers
item.description: House lawmakers have tried to interview Ghislaine Maxwell, but the former girlfriend and confidant of Jeffrey Epstein invoked her Fifth Amendment righ...

item.title: Palestinians say new Israeli measures in West Bank amount to de facto annexation
item.description: The measures aim to increase Israeli control over the occupied West Bank in terms of property law, planning, licensing and enforcement.

item.title: Masks emerge as symbol of Trump’s ICE crackdown and a flashpoint in Congress
The shadowy world of counterfeit wines
item.description: A high-stakes courtroom drama played out in New York City pitting wealthy wine collectors against a man accused of intentionally making and selling mi...

item.title: How much gold is in an Olympic gold medal, and how much is it worth?
item.description: Olympic medals have what's known as a "melt value." But they're worth far more financially than their mineral contents, an auction expert notes.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie makes new video plea as missing mother’s ransom deadline nears
Savannah Guthrie posts new video pleading for help to find mother Nancy
item.description: "Today" show co-host Savannah Guthrie posted a new video Monday pleading for the public's help in the search for her missing mother, Nancy. CBS News' ...

item.title: Why importing more beef from Argentina won't ease costs for U.S. consumers
item.description: Taming runaway U.S. beef prices will require more than stepping up imports, economists said. Here's the key to cutting costs.

item.title: Machine guns to machetes: Weapons that massacred thousands in Iran
item.description: BBC News Persian Forensic has been able to confirm the security forces' deployment of a wide array of lethal and non-lethal weaponry.

item.title: Maxwell refuses to answer questions about Epstein in congressional hearing
item.description: Ghislaine Maxwell, the jailed associate of sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, invoked her Fifth Amendment right to avoid self-incrimination.

item.title: Norway police investigate diplomat over Epstein links
item.description: Mona Juul and her husband Terje Rød-Larsen - who played a vital role in the Oslo Accords - are being investigated for "aggravated corruption".

item.title: Savannah Guthrie pleads for public's help in search for missing mom Nancy
item.description: "Today" show co-host Savannah Guthrie issued a plea for the public's help on Monday at what she called "an hour of desperation" in the search for her ...

item.title: Canadian mining firm says its abducted workers found dead in Mexico
item.description: Vizsla Silver Corp says that family members of some of the kidnapped workers have informed the company that their relatives were found dead.
